proposed_hypotheses.text: The ordinal feature `feature_051` has a monotone negative dose-response with `objective_response`: as `feature_051` increases from 0 to 2, the probability of objective_response decreases.
Higher values of the continuous feature `feature_011` are associated with lower probability of `objective_response`.

analyses.result_summary: Response rate falls monotonically across feature_051 strata: 21.08% (level 0, n=17,557), 15.70% (level 1, n=24,962), 11.05% (level 2, n=7,481). Logistic coef = -0.3749 per unit (OR = 0.687), z = -20.51.
Logistic regression: feature_011 coef = -0.0385 per unit (OR = 0.962), z = -11.87. Quartile response rates: Q1 (0-3.0) = 18.5%, Q2 (3.0-6.4) = 16.3%, Q3-Q4 (>6.4) = 14.2%. Strong inverse association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `feature_013` = 1 have a lower `objective_response` rate than patients with `feature_013` = 0.
Patients with `feature_067` = 1 have a lower `objective_response` rate than patients with `feature_067` = 0.
Patients with `feature_006` = 1 have a higher `objective_response` rate than patients with `feature_006` = 0.

analyses.result_summary: Response rate 15.40% with feature_013=1 (n=32,365) vs 19.64% with feature_013=0 (n=17,635). Risk difference = -4.25 percentage points (chi-square).
Response rate 14.36% with feature_067=1 (n=12,584) vs 17.75% with feature_067=0 (n=37,416). Risk difference = -3.39 percentage points.
Response rate 18.19% with feature_006=1 (n=14,258) vs 16.38% with feature_006=0 (n=35,742). Risk difference = +1.82 percentage points; positive direction confirmed.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher values of the continuous feature `feature_063` are associated with lower `objective_response`.
Higher values of the continuous feature `feature_099` are associated with higher `objective_response`.
Higher values of the continuous feature `feature_092` are associated with higher `objective_response`.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ feature_063', data=df).fit(); also log1p form
analyses.result_summary: feature_063 is right-skewed (median 3.32, max 296.88). On the log(1+x) scale, logistic coef = -0.0713 (OR = 0.931), z = -4.78. Quartile response rates monotonically decrease 18.1% -> 16.0% across Q1->Q4.
Quartile response rates rise monotonically: Q1 (1.7-3.5) 16.0%, Q2 (3.5-3.8) 16.6%, Q3 (3.8-4.1) 17.7%, Q4 (4.1-5.5) 17.7%. t-test responder mean 3.821 vs non 3.798.

analyses.code: ttest_ind responders vs non on feature_092
analyses.result_summary: Responder mean 0.3130 vs non-responder mean 0.3056. Modest positive association; quartile response 16.7%/16.5%/16.5%/17.8%.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patient race (`feature_123`) is associated with `objective_response`: white patients have a lower response rate than non-white patients.
Insurance type (`feature_005`) is associated with `objective_response`: medicaid patients have a lower response rate than privately insured patients.

analyses.code: chi2_contingency on feature_123 x objective_response; logit ~ I(white)
analyses.result_summary: Response rates: white 16.55% (n=32,607), hispanic 17.15%, asian 17.72%, black 17.76%, other 18.54%. Chi-square across 5 levels p=0.045. White vs non-white: coef = -0.0702 (OR = 0.932), z = -2.82, p = 0.005. Direction supports the hypothesis (whites lower).

analyses.code: chi2_contingency on feature_005 x objective_response; logit medicaid vs others
analyses.result_summary: Medicaid 16.13% (n=6,999), medicare 16.85%, uninsured 17.00%, private 17.20%. Overall chi-square p=0.232 (not significant). Medicaid vs all others: coef = -0.0642 (OR = 0.938), z = -1.84, p = 0.066 (marginal). Direction matches but not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Smoking status (`feature_057`) is associated with `objective_response`: never-smokers have a different response rate than ever-smokers (current or former).
Histology (`feature_043`) is associated with `objective_response`: squamous patients have a higher response rate than adenocarcinoma patients.

analyses.result_summary: Response rates: current 17.23%, former 16.85%, never 16.38%. Chi-square across 3 levels p=0.27. Never-smokers numerically lowest but difference small and not significant.
Squamous response rate 17.37% (n=13,901) vs adenocarcinoma 16.71% (n=36,099). Risk difference +0.66 pp, marginally non-significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patient age (`feature_078`, range 30-90) is associated with `objective_response`: older age is associated with lower response.
The binary feature `feature_007` (prevalence 45%) is associated with `objective_response`: patients with feature_007=1 have a higher response rate.
The binary feature `feature_039` (prevalence 50%) is associated with `objective_response`: patients with feature_039=1 have a higher response rate.

analyses.result_summary: Mean age 65.03 in responders vs 64.98 in non-responders; difference 0.05 years. Logistic regression coef ~ 0.0003 per year. No association detected.
Response rate 17.54% with feature_007=1 (n=22,371) vs 16.37% with feature_007=0 (n=27,629). Risk difference +1.16 pp; logistic coef ~ 0.08.
Response rate 17.39% with feature_039=1 (n=25,008) vs 16.40% with feature_039=0 (n=24,992). Risk difference +0.99 pp.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After multivariable adjustment, the apparent racial disparity (white vs non-white, h9) attenuates, suggesting that part of the unadjusted effect on `objective_response` is mediated by clinical features such as `feature_051` (the strong PS-like ordinal predictor) or `feature_011`.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ I(feature_123=="white") + feature_051 + feature_011 + feature_013', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: After adjustment for feature_051, feature_011, and feature_013, the white-vs-non-white coef is -0.0645 (OR = 0.937), z = -2.57, p = 0.010 (vs unadjusted -0.0702). Effect partially attenuated but persists, suggesting only modest confounding by these clinical features.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a comprehensive multivariable logistic model including the leading univariate predictors (feature_011, feature_013, feature_051, feature_067, feature_006, feature_063, feature_099, feature_007, feature_039, feature_092, feature_078, race, insurance, smoking, histology), the strong univariate effects of feature_051, feature_011, feature_013, feature_067, and feature_006 remain independently and significantly associated with `objective_response`.

In the multivariable model from h17, race (`feature_123`) and smoking (`feature_057`) lose statistical significance, indicating their univariate associations are largely confounded by clinical features.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ feature_011 + feature_013 + feature_051 + feature_067 + feature_006 + feature_063 + feature_099 + feature_007 + feature_039 + C(feature_123) + C(feature_005) + C(feature_057) + C(feature_043) + feature_078 + feature_092', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Independent (adjusted) effects, all p<0.001 unless noted: feature_051 -0.382 (OR 0.683), feature_013 -0.302 (OR 0.739), feature_067 -0.259 (OR 0.772), feature_011 -0.0394 per unit, feature_006 +0.130 (OR 1.139), feature_099 +0.093 (OR 1.097), feature_092 +0.174 (OR 1.189), feature_007 +0.081 (p=0.001), feature_039 +0.077 (p=0.001), feature_063 -0.0051 per unit. Race dummies all non-significant (p=0.13-0.94); white vs reference p=0.13. Smoking and histology also non-significant. Insurance: private vs medicaid p=0.019, others NS. Pseudo-R^2 = 0.020. Confirms h17 and h18.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a multiplicative interaction on the log-odds scale between `feature_051` (PS-like ordinal) and `feature_011` (continuous biomarker): the negative effect of feature_011 on `objective_response` is stronger at higher feature_051 levels.
There is a multiplicative interaction on the log-odds scale between `feature_013` and `feature_051`: the additional negative effect of feature_013 differs across feature_051 levels.

analyses.result_summary: Main effects feature_051 -0.365 and feature_011 -0.0367 retained. Interaction term feature_051:feature_011 = -0.0034 (z=-0.68). No detectable interaction.

Main effects feature_013 -0.309, feature_051 -0.385 retained. Interaction term feature_013:feature_051 = +0.013 (z=0.35). No detectable interaction; effects of feature_013 and feature_051 appear additive on the logit scale.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a multiplicative interaction between `feature_006` (positive predictor) and histology (`feature_043`): the favorable effect of feature_006=1 on `objective_response` differs between adenocarcinoma and squamous histology.
The favorable effect of feature_006 on `objective_response` (h5) is preserved across all three feature_051 strata (i.e., it is a robust subgroup-consistent main effect).

analyses.result_summary: Main effect feature_006 = +0.133 in adenocarcinoma. Interaction feature_006:squamous = -0.028, z=-0.50. No detectable histology-dependent modulation of feature_006 effect.

analyses.code: stratified RR within feature_051 levels; smf.logit('objective_response ~ feature_006 * feature_051', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Risk-difference of feature_006=1 vs 0 within strata: feature_051=0 +1.6pp (p=0.023), =1 +2.1pp (p<0.001), =2 +1.6pp (p=0.058). Interaction term feature_006:feature_051 = +0.040, p=0.31 -> not significant; effect direction is consistent across all strata, supporting robustness.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After full multivariable adjustment, the Medicaid disadvantage on `objective_response` (h10) is no longer significant, indicating any apparent SES association is explained by clinical/biomarker case-mix.
Insurance contrasts other than Medicaid (private, medicare, uninsured) show no clinically meaningful difference from one another in `objective_response` after adjustment.

analyses.code: Reading C(feature_005) coefficients from the adjusted model in iteration 8.
analyses.result_summary: With medicaid as reference: medicare +0.063 (p=0.10), private +0.089 (p=0.019), uninsured +0.083 (p=0.23). Only private-vs-medicaid reaches p<0.05; the unadjusted Medicaid signal (univariate p=0.066) is no longer notable once clinical features (feature_051, feature_011, feature_013, feature_067) are included. h23 supported; h24 broadly supported (private slightly higher, medicare and uninsured statistically indistinguishable).

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is an interaction between `feature_007` (positive predictor) and smoking status (`feature_057`): the favorable effect of feature_007=1 on `objective_response` is stronger in current/former smokers than in never-smokers.
There is an interaction between `feature_007` and histology (`feature_043`): the effect of feature_007=1 differs between adenocarcinoma and squamous histology.

analyses.result_summary: Main effect feature_007 in current smokers = +0.097 (p=0.025). Interaction with former = -0.003 (p=0.96), with never = -0.092 (p=0.23). Direction in never-smokers is qualitatively smaller, but no statistically significant interaction.
Main effect feature_007 in adenocarcinoma = +0.074 (p=0.009). Interaction with squamous = +0.031 (z=0.58); no significant histology-by-feature_007 interaction. Effect direction consistent.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A 3-way interaction among `feature_006`, histology (`feature_043`), and smoking (`feature_057`) modifies the response-promoting effect of feature_006.

The continuous biomarker `feature_011` shows the same negative association with `objective_response` within both `feature_013`=0 and `feature_013`=1 strata (no qualitative or quantitative interaction).

analyses.result_summary: All 3-way interaction terms have p > 0.34; the largest is feature_006:squamous:never at -0.291 (p=0.61). 2-way interactions involving feature_006 also non-significant. No evidence of higher-order modulation; feature_006 main effect ~ +0.18 in adeno/current is the largest stratum-specific estimate.

analyses.code: Stratified logit ~ feature_011 within feature_013 strata; smf.logit('objective_response ~ feature_011 * feature_013', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Within feature_013=0: coef = -0.0364 (p=1.3e-12). Within feature_013=1: coef = -0.0405 (p=6.9e-22). Interaction term -0.0041 (p=0.54) -> not significant. Confirms hypothesis: the negative association of feature_011 is similar in both strata.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the 77 binary features screened, only feature_013, feature_067, feature_006, feature_007, and feature_039 reach p < 0.01 in their univariate association with `objective_response`; the remaining 72 binaries show no reliable individual signal.

The dominant predictor structure for `objective_response` in this NSCLC cohort is a small set (~5-8) of clinical/biomarker variables (feature_051, feature_011, feature_013, feature_067, feature_006, feature_063, feature_099, feature_092) acting essentially additively on the log-odds scale, with no detectable two-way or three-way interaction among the variables tested.

analyses.code: Univariate chi-square / t-test screen of all 123 features stored in _uni.csv; count features with p<0.01.
analyses.result_summary: From the screen: feature_013 (p=1.1e-33), feature_067 (p=2.0e-18), feature_006 (p=1.0e-6), feature_007 (p=5.9e-4), feature_039 (p=3.4e-3) are the only binary features below p=0.01. feature_076 (p=0.019), feature_112 (p=0.033), feature_021 (p=0.051) are next. Of 77 binaries, exactly 5 reach p<0.01 - hypothesis confirmed.

analyses.code: Synthesizing iterations 8-13: full multivariable model + interaction tests across feature_051, feature_011, feature_013, feature_006, feature_007, histology, smoking.
analyses.result_summary: All ten 2-way and 3-way interaction tests carried out (feature_051xfeature_011, feature_013xfeature_051, feature_006xfeature_043, feature_006xfeature_051, feature_007xfeature_057, feature_007xfeature_043, feature_011xfeature_013, feature_006xfeature_043xfeature_057) returned p > 0.23. The fully adjusted multivariable model reproduces univariate signs and magnitudes with minimal attenuation. Cohort-level signal is well-described as additive on the logit scale with main effects of ~8 features. Pseudo-R^2 = 0.020 indicates substantial residual unexplained variation.
